investigation_summary: Documented issues cluster in offshore supply-chain labor (audit gaming cited by NGOs), 2018 gender-discrimination class settlement ($13M), transfer-pricing / IRS disclosure risk, and sustainability claims vs verified progress. No criminal conviction in this record set.

profile.tax_summary: FY2023 effective rate ~18.4% vs 21% statutory. Bermuda / Dutch structures documented for IP royalties. SEC disclosures note IRS transfer-pricing dispute with potential ~$1.4B liability. Oregon incentives historically $2B+ (Good Jobs First).
profile.legal_summary: 2018: gender discrimination class action — $13M settlement; investigation described systemic promotion bias; executive exits. 2019: harassment-related executive departures. Ongoing cotton / Xinjiang forced-labor litigation allegations. 2023: FTC scrutiny of influencer disclosures.
profile.labor_summary: Good On You: It's a Start (3/5). Manufacturing concentrated Vietnam, Indonesia, China; NGO critiques of audit quality. No supply-chain living-wage certification. Historical sweatshop era led to programs; critics say gaps remain. U.S. warehouses: high injury rates reported. PFAS detected in garments (third-party tests 2022–2024).
profile.environmental_summary: CDP climate B (2023). Move to Zero; scope 3 remains hard. Flyknit reduces cut waste vs traditional cut-and-sew. PFAS environmental persistence concerns parallel product testing.
profile.political_summary: ~$800K federal lobbying (2023). Colin Kaepernick deal (2018) — commercial/cultural, not legal. Oregon HQ in business-friendly tax climate.
profile.product_health: Athletic wear typical profile. PFAS in technical fabrics under scrutiny in third-party testing. No major recalls emphasized in this volume.
profile.executive_summary: Phil Knight: very large disclosed philanthropy (e.g., University of Oregon, Stanford); no corruption findings in this record. Recent CEOs: strategic and stock-performance commentary in press.
